input.input: You are the PDF workflows specialist. Work in the project tree and produce deliverables under /_outputs/pdf_task_output. Inputs: two PDFs at /test_demo/pdfs/attention.pdf and /test_demo/pdfs/gpt4.pdf. Task: 1) Convert each entire PDF to a single text file (not images) and save as: /_outputs/pdf_task_output/attention.txt and /_outputs/pdf_task_output/gpt4.txt. Ensure the text conversion preserves order and includes page breaks markers. 2) Extract all tables and numeric result tables; save each table as CSV under /_outputs/pdf_task_output/tables/, named to indicate source (e.g., attention_table1.csv, gpt4_table2.csv). 3) For each paper, produce a detailed analysis markdown file containing the following sections: - Core ideas and contributions - Architecture and design choices (technical structure) - Training methodology and data usage (if present) - Evaluation setup, benchmarks, and performance metrics - Key numerical results and tables (embed or reference extracted CSVs) - Limitations, weaknesses, and assumptions - Short machine-readable JSON summary with fields for each section (save as /_outputs/pdf_task_output/attention_summary.json and gpt4_summary.json). Save the markdowns as /_outputs/pdf_task_output/attention_analysis.md and /_outputs/pdf_task_output/gpt4_analysis.md. 4) Produce a comparative analysis markdown that addresses: - Evolution from attention-based models to GPT-4 level systems - Architectural and methodological changes across the two papers - Differences in scaling strategies, performance improvements, and design philosophy - Key innovations introduced and what was replaced or improved over time Save as /_outputs/pdf_task_output/comparative_analysis.md. 5) Produce a combined detailed research report synthesizing both papers, suitable for an expert reader (4-8 pages equivalent), referencing extracted tables and summarizing implications for future work. Save as /_outputs/pdf_task_output/combined_research_report.md. 6) Create a merged PDF containing both documents in a unified format (concatenate and normalize) at /_outputs/pdf_task_output/merged_documents.pdf. Constraints and notes: - Do NOT convert each page to an image; produce text conversions. - Read the entire PDFs. - When extracting tables, ensure numeric fidelity (commas, decimals) and include a brief note if extraction had uncertainty. - Use as few files as practical but include the files above. - Return only: the list of output file paths and a short (2-3 sentence) summary of what you produced. Do not include file contents in your reply. If you encounter pages that are scanned images or OCR is needed, perform OCR and note it in the short summary. Start work and write files under /_outputs/pdf_task_output as specified.

Summary: I extracted full-text (with page-break markers) for both PDFs, extracted all detected tables as CSVs (kept numeric strings intact; any ambiguous cells were left as-is and noted in the CSVs), produced detailed per-paper analyses and JSON summaries, a comparative analysis, a combined expert research report, and a merged PDF concatenating the two source documents. No full-page scanned-image OCR was required for the main text extraction; if you want OCR for image-only figure text I can re-run OCR (requires Tesseract/pdf2image) and add OCR notes.
